input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text:
[0-6] The video opens with a close-up of three men in military garrison caps against a dark background. The camera zooms out to reveal a total of five men wearing leather flight jackets. The central figure wears a dark cap with light piping and a jacket with a circular bird emblem patch. To his left, a man wears a jacket with a diamond-shaped patch, while the man on the far left displays a similar bird emblem.
[6-10] The scene cuts to a grainy, high-contrast interior where a figure in light clothing climbs a ladder structure. This transitions to an exterior airfield setting where a uniformed officer with a peaked cap stands near a large aircraft propeller, with a group of personnel walking on the tarmac in the background.
[10-14] White text overlays appear over the nose of a large bomber aircraft, reading "Doolittle Raid" and "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Do." The text updates to include the full name "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Doolittle" and the date "April 18, 1942" as the view shifts to the aircraft's wing and engine area.

[14-18] The camera focuses on a large aircraft engine and propeller assembly. Several individuals in dark uniforms stand beneath the wing structure; one figure reaches upward toward the blades. A white text overlay identifies the "Doolittle Raid" and "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Doolittle" before disappearing from the lower left corner.
[18-26] New text states "At 8:20 a.m., Sixteen B-25B Mitchell Bombers Were Launched from the Deck of the USS Hornet." The scene shifts to a high-angle view of bombers parked along the flight deck, then cuts to a bomber near the edge with choppy ocean waves in the background. A crew member stands with an arm raised, signaling. The text overlay eventually vanishes.
[26-28] The camera cuts to a low-angle shot of the flight deck surface with aircraft landing gear wheels visible at the top. White text reading "MISSION: Strike T" appears in the lower left corner.

[28-32] A low-angle perspective captures black landing gear wheels on a gray flight deck with visible markings while the white text "MISSION: Strike Targets in and Around Tokyo" is displayed at the bottom. The wheels lift off the deck surface, the text vanishes, and the tail section marked "283" becomes clearly visible.
[32-36] The twin-engine bomber ascends with extended landing gear, showing a star insignia against a bright, overcast sky. As the aircraft shrinks flying over the gray, choppy ocean, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear in the foreground right.
[36-42] Historical footage cuts to a modern color interview featuring an elderly, bald man with light skin wearing a dark collared shirt, his mouth partially open. He sits indoors before a wooden shelf holding books and a decorative plate. White text appears reading "Lt. Richard E. Cole," then expands to include "Co-Pilot, Crew No. 1" as he looks slightly off-camera.

[42-46] An elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt sits indoors before a wooden shelf, speaking while the white text "Lt. Richard E. Cole" and "Co-Pilot, Crew No. 1" remains static in the lower left. A dissolve transition begins, blending his translucent face with archival footage. [46-50] The view resolves to a high-angle shot of an aircraft carrier flight deck where multiple twin-engine bombers are parked in rows, secured by white braces. Personnel in dark uniforms move across the gray deck near the railing, with choppy ocean waves in the background. The camera zooms in on the dark aircraft bodies, highlighting white star insignias. [50-56] The scene cuts to a medium shot of a single twin-engine bomber taxiing forward-left on the wet deck. Its landing gear rolls along the surface as propellers blur with motion. The aircraft advances toward the camera, bringing the nose and cockpit into focus, where a pilot is faintly visible behind the glass.

[56-60] A twin-engine bomber taxis on a flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit close to the lens before the view shifts to the left wing and fuselage top with the pilot visible. The aircraft continues leftward past the camera, revealing the mid-fuselage, wing, and tail section with blurred propellers against the ocean.
[60-62] A dissolve transition blends the bomber's tail with a modern color interview, superimposing an elderly man's face as the black-and-white footage fades to show him speaking with eyes closed.
[62-66] The scene cuts to black-and-white archival footage of uniformed figures running down a city street carrying large leafy trees, followed by a high-angle view of a dense city skyline with rectangular buildings.
[66-70] The footage cuts to a low-angle shot of a dark twin-engine bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended against a bright sky, then flying low over a blurry landscape with propellers blurring as it approaches ground level.

recent_thinks: [70-72] The camera cuts to a grainy, frontal close-up of the bomber's nose section. The rounded glass cockpit is central, flanked by dark wings extending horizontally. The footage focuses tightly on the front fuselage structure as the aircraft maintains its flight path, obscuring the previous landscape background with a plain, light surface.

[72-74] The grainy, black-and-white footage maintains a tight, frontal close-up on the bomber's nose section as it flies forward. The rounded glass cockpit area dominates the center of the frame, flanked by the dark, horizontal extensions of the wings against a plain, light sky background. The aircraft appears to move slightly closer to the lens.

[74-76] The scene cuts from the close-up of the aircraft's nose to a cylindrical object falling vertically through the air. The light-colored bomb descends amidst billowing white smoke or clouds against a dark, grainy sky background, appearing small in the frame.

[76-78] The cylindrical object is visible near the top of the frame, falling through the sky. It appears smaller against the billowing white clouds. Other small specks are scattered in the background, suggesting multiple objects descending through the gray atmosphere.

[78-80] A light-colored cylindrical bomb falls vertically through a cloudy, gray sky. The object descends from the upper frame, surrounded by billowing white clouds. It moves lower into the center of the view against the atmospheric background.

[80-82] A light-colored cylindrical bomb continues falling vertically through a cloudy sky. The object descends from the middle area of the frame toward the bottom center. Billowing white and gray clouds surround the falling item as it drops further down against the atmospheric background.
